relations.note: Email-specific avoidance guidance narrows the general Coral usage restriction.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Maintain consistent visual weighting of brand colors across executions.
rules.rule_text: Apply the brand palette in a primary > secondary > tertiary ratio: the Brand Blue and Gold family primaries/secondaries dominate a layout, while tertiary colors (Coral, Golden Sand, Graphite, Light Blue, White) are used only as accents and surfaces.

quotes: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).

rules.intent: Ensure headline legibility and brand consistency across background types.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headings use Brand Blue (#00529b) as their color (headline.color), switching to White on dark or gradient backgrounds.

quotes: Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Preserve contrast and readability of body copy on both light and dark backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Body text uses Graphite (#212121, body.color) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds, with an increased body line height (body.line_height) applied for dark-background contexts per §1.2.

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Preserve a clear visual hierarchy in charts and reserve gold as an emphasis device for critical data.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) are the dominant data colors (chart.primary.color / chart.data.secondary_color), gray denotes placebo/comparator data, and Sunshine/Gold (#ffc60a) is strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures — it must never be used as a general chart color.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate callout content types via consistent tinted surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue at 70% tint (#E6F0F9 @ 70%) for data callouts and Golden Sand at 30% tint (#FEF1C8 @ 30%) for text-heavy callouts.

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

rules.intent: Prevent Coral from displacing primary/secondary brand colors in key visual elements.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.

quotes: **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Default email compositions to primary/secondary brand colors, reserving Coral for briefed exceptions.
rules.rule_text: In email sections, avoid using Coral unless the creative brief specifically calls for it.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Ensure links remain visually distinguishable and brand-consistent across background types.
rules.rule_text: Links use Brand Blue (#00529b), underlined, on light backgrounds, switching to underlined White on dark backgrounds (body.link.color / body.link.decoration).

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Preserve consistent, recognizable rendering of the logo mark across all applications.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Gold (Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a) must be maintained in all instances, including when the mark is used as a pattern or graphic.
